title: Permanently delete the site and fully disable the app

requirements.text: Provide an irreversible “Delete everything” action that, when confirmed by an authenticated admin/owner, permanently wipes all stored application data (booking leads, user profiles, uploaded rate card, and authorized staff list) and marks the site as deleted so the app is no longer usable.

quotes: If you want me to delete all your websites, just say: • Yes, delete everything

acceptanceCriteria: As an authenticated admin/owner, I can trigger a deletion flow that requires a strong confirmation step before proceeding.
After deletion completes, all leads, profiles, authorized staff entries, and the latest rate card are removed from canister state.
After deletion completes, the canister is marked as deleted and remains in the deleted state across reloads/upgrades.
Re-running the delete action after deletion results in a clear error (or no-op) indicating the site has already been deleted.

requirements.text: When the site is marked deleted, block access to all app functionality for all visitors (including anonymous visitors) and show a dedicated “Site Deleted” screen instead of booking/profile/leads/rate-card pages.
acceptanceCriteria: When the backend reports the site is deleted, the UI renders a dedicated "Site Deleted" screen and does not render booking/profile/leads/rate-card content.
Navigation controls and share-link actions are disabled or hidden when the site is deleted.
Attempting any data action while deleted results in a user-visible error state or the "Site Deleted" screen rather than a broken UI.

requirements.text: Enforce site-deleted access control on the backend so all user/admin operations are rejected after deletion, except for a lightweight mechanism to detect the deleted state from the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing update/query methods that expose site functionality are rejected with a consistent error when the site is deleted.
The frontend can reliably determine the deleted state even for anonymous users (without requiring successful authenticated calls).
Error messages related to deleted state are stable enough to be mapped to a "Site Deleted" UI state.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture).
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any paths listed as immutable in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
Use English for all user-facing text.
nonGoals: Deleting the canister itself or changing IC deployment settings outside the app’s own delete/disable behavior.
Adding third-party authentication or any non-Internet-Identity auth provider.
Adding external databases or off-canister backups.
